Critical — act now
Downtown Funding and Records Search Search BID assessments, DDA grants, TIF reimbursements, board meetings, and financial records in one place.
Annual adopted budget — C.R.S. 29-1-101 et seq · April 10, 2026
Open the search portal →
ADA Title II deadline: April 24, 2026 — 16 days. All local government websites must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Zero district sites are confirmed compliant on PDF documents.
HB21-1110 · 28 C.F.R. Part 35 · April 8, 2026
Open district audit dashboard →
249 of 284 metro districts have no website. CORA request is the only way a resident can access their own district's records.
EPC Assessor audit · April 2026
See district audit →
WS Districts (WSDM) serves 30+ districts on a single WordPress site. Last calendar entry: 2021. No governance docs. No financials. One compliance letter fixes all 30+ simultaneously.
Platform audit · April 2026
Draft compliance letter →
High priority
All posted PDFs likely fail WCAG 1.3.1. Meeting minutes, budgets, and audit docs are almost certainly untagged. This is the real HB21-1110 failure — not the homepage.
ADA document audit · April 2026
View ADA analysis →
0 of 8 audited sites post their service plan. C.R.S. §32-1-202 requires it. This is the foundational legal document. Not one district links it publicly.
Governance gap · §32-1-202
See audit dashboard →
Annual audit documents: publicly posted by 0 of 8 districts. Required by §32-1-106. Due to State Auditor by July 31. The clock is running.
Governance gap · §32-1-106 · due July 31
See audit dashboard →
Informational
2 CORA requests pending response. Military housing numbers and district governance records both awaiting reply. Response window is still open.
CORA tracker · April 2026
View tracker →
Wolfersberger compliance and Bellamese analysis complete. New findings ready for review on developer agreements, compliance history, and parcel-level records.
Compliance analysis · April 2026
Read analysis →
Jurisdiction finder is live. Enter any El Paso County address to find your metro districts, school district, city jurisdiction, and utilities — plain language, no login.
New · April 2026
Find my jurisdiction →
CSPD Blotter — Weekly History is live. 271 incidents pulled directly from the live blotter feed, paired with a Poisson 95% confidence band to flag statistical anomalies week to week. Tabs are full APG-pattern keyboard accessible; every chart has an aria-label.
New · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open blotter dashboard →
Lorson Ranch financial dashboard is live. Seven cross-pledged metro districts, $94.2M aggregate audited deficit, IGA money flows, and a per-home tax calculator — sourced from 2026 budgets and 2024 audits. Companion plain-language briefing available as a download.
New · Special Districts · April 2026
Open Lorson Ranch dashboard →
CBI Missing Persons form — accessible rebuild is live. Resolves all seven critical/serious findings from the ADA audit: skip link, live regions, focus management, EN/ES localization, real-time validation messaging. Drops into the existing Drupal Webform infrastructure with optional Claude API routing.
New · State · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open CBI form rebuild →
LexisNexis ADAM Program dashboard — accessible rebuild is live. Audit and reference rebuild of the missing-children dashboard: APG-pattern tabs with roving tabindex and full keyboard nav, solid amber focus rings (3:1 against any surface), darker success-green for AA contrast, and "View Poster" placeholders converted from dead anchors to real buttons.
New · Private Sector · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open ADAM dashboard →

El Paso County · Colorado Springs · Plain language

Who's in charge
of your address?

Type your address. Find out who governs it — city, county, school district, utilities — and get direct links to the right map. One box. No jargon.

Try an example

Demonstration using sample data — no address is transmitted or stored.

Your address
Lookup complete
Who governs this address

Free map tools for this area
Property assessment · Taxes · Protest
El Paso County Assessor handles property values and tax assessment for all addresses in the county — city or unincorporated. Protest deadline: May 1st in odd-numbered years.
County Assessor →

No results found

Try a shorter version of your address — for example 123 Main St without the city name.
Contact the Assessor: assessor@elpasoco.com  ·  719-520-6600

Incorporated
Inside a city or town with its own government, roads, zoning, and services. Colorado Springs, Manitou Springs, Fountain, and Monument are all incorporated.
Unincorporated
Within El Paso County but not inside any city. The county governs directly — no city council, no city zoning, no city utilities. Very common in rural and exurban areas.
Jurisdiction
Which government has authority over your address. Depending on what you're asking about, two or more governments may have jurisdiction at once — over different things.
Special district
A separate unit of government that provides one specific service — water, fire, sanitation, utilities. Your address may be in several special districts at once, each with its own tax levy.
Mill levy
The property tax rate. One mill = $1 of tax per $1,000 of assessed value. Your total mill levy combines county, city/town, school district, and all special district levies.
Assessed value
The value the county assigns for tax purposes. In Colorado, residential assessed value is 6.765% of actual (market) value — set by state law, not the county.
Valuation protest
Your legal right to challenge the Assessor's value estimate. You may file every two years. Deadline: May 1st in odd-numbered years. Free. No lawyer required.

Not looking for a specific address? These are the best free tools for El Paso County parcels, zoning, and property maps — all county or state maintained, all free.

Free parcel & map tools
EPC Parcels Open Data El Paso County GIS Division Official parcel boundaries. ArcGIS viewer. GeoJSON, Shapefile, and CSV download. API access included. County-owned · Free Colorado Parcel Viewer Colorado Division of Property Taxation Statewide parcel map. Assessment data across all 64 Colorado counties in one place. State-maintained · Free County Zoning Map Viewer El Paso County Planning Planning and zoning layers, parcel boundaries, development overlay zones. Includes city limits. County-owned · Free Free GIS Data Downloads El Paso County GIS Division Bulk shapefiles, tax entity boundaries, aerial photography. For developers and researchers. County-owned · Free

City roads · Utilities · City council sites
Federal complaint
ada.gov/file-a-complaint
Free · No lawyer required · Both county and city
